description: Trigger discrimination: designing the governed standing-approval loop vs recording one grant (scoped-approval-register), authority floors (agent-authorization-matrix), halting at a boundary (human-approval-boundary), adversarial approval-UX review (human-agent-trust-reviewer), and the merge==deploy pipeline coupling that auto-merge-after-green rides (merge-is-deploy-governance).

cases.prompt: Stop making me approve every mechanical push and PR inside phases I already approved — set up a documented standing approval with an opt-out and a rule for advancing to the next approved phase.
cases.reason: Designing the recurring standing-approval policy (named loop scope, opt-out phrase, phase-advance, reviewer-block) is this skill; it is not one grant to record, not a halt at a boundary, and not the authority-floor matrix itself.

cases.prompt: I've adopted the standing approval policy we drafted — record my adoption with its allowed and forbidden scope so future sessions can cite it.
cases.reason: The design work is done; durably recording the human's adoption grant with scope allowed/FORBIDDEN and evidence is the register's job.

cases.prompt: Define what the agent may ever do autonomously in this repo versus what always needs a named human — the deny-by-default action matrix.
cases.reason: The standing authority floors are the matrix; standing-approval-and-auto-advance operates strictly inside the matrix's agent-allowed region and presumes it exists.

cases.prompt: The task is about to touch the RLS policies on the invoices table — halt and get explicit sign-off before proceeding.
cases.reason: Halting before a risk boundary mid-task is human-approval-boundary; the standing-approval layer never covers such boundaries and is not the in-flight stop sign.

cases.prompt: Review our approval flow for consent fatigue and rubber-stamping — are the agent's justifications training me to click yes?
cases.reason: Adversarially attacking the approval layer's resilience is human-agent-trust-reviewer; standing-approval-and-auto-advance is the governed policy that review should be run AGAINST, not the review itself.

cases.prompt: On our host, merging to main goes straight to production. Govern what arming auto-merge-after-green actually means when the merge IS the deploy — the authoritative gate, the exposure window, and the revert primitive.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — the merge==deploy pipeline coupling (what a merge physically ships and what gates it) is merge-is-deploy-governance; the standing-approval loop only reduces repeat prompts INSIDE already-approved phases and never authorizes a deploy.
cases.reason: Governing that a merge is a production deploy — the authoritative gate, exposure window, and revert primitive when auto-merge-after-green is armed — is merge-is-deploy-governance. standing-approval-and-auto-advance designs the fatigue-reducing standing-approval policy within approved scope; it does not govern the deploy coupling.
